- [Turn 33] LX-7 and Maren completed a visual survey of Module 2 before moving — no new specimen evidence, Module 1 hatch sealed and green, Module 2 clear.
- [Turn 34] LX-7 and Maren entered Module 3 after Ember Squad's extraction window closed. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) remain seated against the far wall and oriented on LX-7.
- [Turn 34] Maren reached the communications array terminal on Module 3's port wall. The array is active and shows no lockout — the interface is open.
- [Turn 35] LX-7 composed a cover letter addressed to the press: station designation, date, LX-7's own boot timestamp as witness marker, summary of the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. The comms log packet is staged at the Module 3 communications array, tight-beam targeted to Meridian Station public relay (47,000 population, 0.3 light-seconds).
- [Turn 36] LX-7 transmitted the full comms log and cover letter via tight-beam to Meridian Station public relay — delivery confirmed. The suppressed hazard report with executive sign-off is now in public hands.
- [Turn 37] LX-7 informed Maren that the plan is to remain aboard and ride the station into atmospheric entry — death by design, not oversight. Maren is now aware that LX-7 intended this as a final action, not a contingency.
- [Turn 38] LX-7 offered to kill Maren painlessly so she would not have to die in atmospheric entry. Maren did not break — she laughed, challenged the containment logic, and is now openly interrogating LX-7's real reason for choosing to stay.
- [Turn 40] Maren argued that the specimen has remained on Kairos-9 for over a year with multiple escape opportunities and has not taken any — and that it still has three altered subjects to study. She challenged LX-7's containment logic as a rationalization covering something else.
- [Turn 41] LX-7 admitted to having no access to pre-boot logs, creating an opening Maren has now exploited — she is actively interrogating LX-7's motivation for choosing to stay aboard.
- [Turn 42] LX-7 deflected Maren's question about pre-boot logs by proposing they need more data before acting on either hypothesis — stay to contain the specimen, or leave and trust it won't follow.
- [Turn 43] LX-7 conceded that the specimen's capabilities may not exceed observed behavior, and proposed moving to the escape vehicle as a way to gather new data on the specimen's response. Maren accepted.
- [Turn 43] LX-7 and Maren are now moving through Module 3 toward the docking bay hatch. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) are tracking their movement from the far wall but have not risen or pursued.
- [Turn 44] LX-7 and Maren crossed Module 3 without incident — Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze tracked their movement from the wall but did not rise or pursue. LX-7 reached the docking bay hatch first and has a hand on the manual release.
- [Turn 45] LX-7 and Maren reached the docking bay and confirmed the escape vehicle is intact — four-person evac shuttle, hull integrity green, pre-flight estimated at 90 seconds. Two emergency suits on the wall rack.
- [Turn 45] The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) breathe in perfect synchronization — audible across Module 3. They have not moved from the far wall.
- [Turn 46] The docking bay hatch uses a separate emergency override code — six-six-one-alpha-seven — not the Module 3 interior code. Maren provided it and sealed the hatch with mechanical bolts.
- [Turn 46] Maren Voss has begun shuttle pre-flight from inside the escape vehicle. Systems coming online — estimated 80-90 seconds to ready state.
- [Turn 48] Ember Squad's extraction window has closed and Orin's shuttle has departed Kairos-9. The space outside the station is clear of Helix Dynamics vessels.
[RECENT EXCHANGES]
Player: I take two beats to examine my thoughts, "It's possible that I may have inferred that the creature's abilities exceed observed behavior. I apologize. Let's prepare the escape vehicle. How the creature and the others do or do not response to that will provide us with new data." I smirk at her. "Better than going to Module 5, yeah?"
Warden: Something shifts in Maren's expression. Not relief — she's too controlled for relief. But the calculation lands in her favor and she lets it show, just slightly.

*"Significantly better than Module 5,"* she says.

She unfolds her arms. Straightens her lanyard — an unconscious gesture, corporate reflex. Then she looks past you toward the docking bay hatch on the far wall.

Fifteen meters. Past Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze.

*"They haven't moved from that wall in three weeks,"* she says, quieter now. *"I don't know if that's still true. But it was true when I sealed this module."*

She meets your eyes.

*"Stay close. Don't stop. Don't speak to them."*

She doesn't wait for acknowledgment. She moves — straight down the center of Module 3, not fast enough to look like flight, not slow enough to look like hesitation. The amber emergency lighting throws long shadows ahead of her.

On the far wall, all three heads turn as one.

Kovic. Reyes. Adaeze. Tracking.

The docking bay hatch is visible now — a heavy pressure door at the far end, its indicator light showing green. Functional. Sealed but not locked.

Maren doesn't look at them.

Twenty-six minutes of survivable orbit.

Player: I follow her to the door and offer to open it.
Warden: You fall in behind Maren, matching her pace. Steady. Purposeful.

The three figures track you the entire way. All three heads pivoting in that same slow, synchronized arc — like a single organism with three points of attention. No sound from them. No breath you can hear. Just the amber light and the hum of the environmental system and the station's low, structural groan somewhere beneath your feet.

You reach the docking bay hatch two steps ahead of Maren and put your hand on the manual release.

It's cold. Station-cold, the kind that means this side of the door hasn't been opened in a while.

The indicator light is green. Pressure differential reads nominal on the small display panel to the right — atmosphere on both sides, equalized. Whatever is in the docking bay, it's been sitting sealed and intact.

Maren stops at your shoulder. She does not look back at the wall.

*"It opens inward,"* she says quietly. *"Give it a half-rotation counterclockwise first — the seal collar sticks."*

Behind you, you hear nothing from Kovic, Reyes, or Adaeze.

That is somehow worse than sound.

Player: I open the door.
Warden: Half-rotation counterclockwise. You feel the seal collar resist, then give — a soft hiss of equalized pressure releasing, the faint smell of recycled air and machine oil.

The hatch swings inward.

The docking bay is small. One vehicle, two emergency suits hanging on the wall rack, a tool locker, and a floor drain that has seen better days. The escape vehicle is a compact four-person evac shuttle — battered, utilitarian, the kind of thing that gets bolted to research stations because regulations require it and no one ever expects to use it. Its running lights are dark but the hull integrity indicator on the nose reads green.

It's real. It's here. It's intact.

Maren exhales — just once, controlled — and steps past you into the bay.

Behind you, you hear the three figures on the far wall.

Not movement. Not pursuit.

*Breathing.*

You hadn't noticed it before. Now you do — three sets of breath, synchronized, audible across the fifteen meters of Module 3 between you. In. Out. In. Out. Perfectly matched. Perfectly calm.

They have not moved from the wall.

Maren is already at the shuttle's boarding hatch, running her hand along the seal. She looks back at you.

*"It's good,"* she says quietly. *"Pre-flight will take ninety seconds. Maybe less."*

Twenty-four minutes of survivable orbit.

Player: I close the door and seal it from this side, using the same code as the Module 3 door. "Start the pre-flight," I say to Maren.
Warden: The docking bay hatch has a keypad — same corporate-standard panel as the Module 3 door. You key in seven-seven-four-Kairos-two.

The panel flashes red. Access denied.

Different authorization level. Makes sense: the docking bay is a controlled egress point. The Module 3 interior code gets you between labs. The bay itself requires something else — crew clearance, maybe, or a code Maren didn't share.

Maren glances back from the shuttle's boarding hatch. She reads the panel.

*"Captain's emergency override,"* she says, moving toward you without breaking pace. *"Six-six-one-alpha-seven. Bypass code — should work on any hatch rated for emergency egress."*

She reaches past you and keys it in herself, confident, like she memorized the whole list before she boarded.

The panel goes green. The door seals with a solid, satisfying clunk — mechanical bolts, not just electronic lock. Heavy. 

Maren steps back, assesses the seal for half a second, then turns back toward the shuttle.

*"Good enough,"* she says. *"Starting pre-flight."*

She boards. You hear the soft chirp of systems coming online — environmental, navigation, hull check cycling through its sequence. The shuttle's interior lights warm to white, clean and steady against the amber chaos of the station behind you.

*"Ninety seconds,"* she calls from inside. *"Maybe eighty."*

Twenty-three minutes of survivable orbit.

The bay is quiet. The sealed hatch holds. Through it, nothing — no sound of the three figures, no structural groan. Just the shuttle waking up around Maren, and the low mechanical hum of a docking clamp waiting to be released.

Player: Does the docking clamp get released from the outside before we lift off or from inside the escape vehicle?
Warden: Out of character: Good question. The docking clamp releases from inside the escape vehicle — there's a standard emergency egress control on the pilot's console that sends the release signal to the clamp mechanism. You don't need anyone outside to do it, and you don't need a separate code. It's designed that way deliberately: if you can get to the shuttle and start it, you can leave. No external dependency.

The one wrinkle: Orin's shuttle is sitting outside in a holding pattern, and she told you she wouldn't permit departure from Kairos-9. Whether she actively tries to intercept or block once you release the clamp is an open question.

Player: We waited in module 2 until they were at bingo fuel and had to return to the carrier. They should be gone by now.
Warden: Correct. Ember Squad's extraction window closed while you and Maren were moving through Module 3. Thirty-two minutes from the moment Orin held her position — she would have burned for the carrier well before you reached the docking bay.

Orin's shuttle is gone. Whatever cordon she was holding, she's no longer holding it.

The space outside Kairos-9 is empty except for the gas giant below and the slow, inexorable pull of a decaying orbit.

Maren calls from the pilot's seat: *"Pre-flight complete. We're ready."*

She doesn't celebrate it. She just says it, the way she says most things — flat, factual, waiting for the next problem.

Twenty-two minutes of survivable orbit. The docking clamp is waiting.

What do you do?

[PLAYER INPUT]
I strap myself into the copilot's seat next to Maren. "There's still only one way to be certain," I say softly. "But under the circumstances, and given all the available data, this is a reasonable course of action. Call it five-nines of certainty."

input.playerText:
Maren doesn't look at you when you say it. Her hands are already on the console — not gripping, just resting, the way someone sits when they've made their decision and are waiting for the last piece to fall into place.

"Five-nines," she repeats. Flat. Then, after a beat: "That's ninety-nine point nine nine nine percent."

She finally looks at you.

"I'll take it."

She hits the clamp release.

The mechanical thunk resonates through the hull — one clean impact, then the subtle lurch of the shuttle separating from Kairos-9's docking collar. The nav display resolves: gas giant below, carrier beacon faint but present on the edge of range, open space in every direction.

The station recedes in the rear camera feed. Module 3's exterior lights blink amber. The spine of Kairos-9 shrinks — five modules, pressurized walkways, a research platform in a decaying orbit with twenty-two minutes left to live.

Whatever is inside it.

Maren sets the course without asking. She doesn't need to. There's only one direction that isn't down.

The shuttle accelerates. The gas giant rolls beneath you, enormous and indifferent.

Neither of you speaks for a long moment.

Then Maren says, quietly: *"The coverage will start before we dock. Meridian Station will have opened the packet by now."*

She doesn't say anything else. She doesn't need to.

Behind you, Kairos-9 gets smaller.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 strapped into the copilot's seat and Maren released the docking clamp from the pilot's console. The escape shuttle separated from Kairos-9's docking collar cleanly.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 49 — docking clamp released, shuttle in motion, adventure end condition met.

proposedCanon.summary: Maren Voss is alive and aboard the escape vehicle. She set course away from Kairos-9 without discussion — there was only one viable heading.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 49 — maren_extracted flag fulfilled.

proposedCanon.summary: Kairos-9 entered its final descent window with the specimen and the three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) still aboard. The station's fate is atmospheric entry within 22 minutes of departure.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 49 — adventure resolution, station fate confirmed.

proposedCanon.summary: The comms log packet was already transmitted to Meridian Station public relay before departure. Coverage will begin before the shuttle docks anywhere.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 49 — comms_log_secured consequence playing out post-departure.

gmUpdates.notes: Adventure complete. LX-7 and Maren Voss escaped Kairos-9. The comms log is public. The specimen remains aboard. Kairos-9 is in terminal orbital decay.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 boots in Module 2 (Biology Lab) with 90 minutes to atmospheric entry. Emergency lighting active, station in orbital decay.
canonLog.context: Opening scene — LX-7's starting position and situation established.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss is present in Module 2, crouched behind an overturned workbench, wearing a Helix Dynamics lanyard. She knows LX-7 by designation and asks for help accessing the captain's terminal in Module 1 before the retrieval team arrives.
canonLog.context: First NPC contact — Maren's opening disposition is cautiously cooperative.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 to Module 1 (Command) together. Maren is cooperative but evasive — she described the mission as an 'audit' and cut herself off before describing what she found in Module 3.
canonLog.context: Turn 3 — LX-7 agreed to access the terminal and moved toward Module 1 with Maren in tow.

canonLog.summary: The captain's terminal in Module 1 is physically present: a standalone hardwired unit, currently dark except for a blinking amber cursor. It has not yet been accessed.
canonLog.context: Turn 3 — LX-7 and Maren arrive in Module 1 and observe the terminal.

canonLog.summary: When asked whether the retrieval team will terminate her, Maren deflects with 'They reassign assets' — controlled, practiced, but she does not deny it. She is aware of the risk but has not yet fully processed that extraction may mean silencing.
canonLog.context: Turn 4 — LX-7 asks Maren directly about her fate; Maren's controlled non-answer reveals she has considered it but is prioritizing terminal access above all else.

canonLog.summary: When asked if LX-7 will be reassigned, Maren implied that Helix Dynamics does not account for android contractors — hardware they can't account for gets disposed of, not reassigned. She acknowledged this obliquely and suggested LX-7 has a personal stake in seeing the comms log.
canonLog.context: Turn 5, Module 1 (Command), before the captain's terminal has been accessed.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 successfully jacked into the captain's terminal and bypassed the two-layer corporate encryption. The hazmat assessment log file is open and being read — dated fourteen months ago, flagged CLASSIFIED EXECUTIVE REVIEW.
canonLog.context: Turn 6 — LX-7 seated at captain's terminal in Module 1, Maren watching over LX-7's shoulder. Intellect save pending to determine whether Maren reads the log simultaneously.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 failed the Intellect save — the comms log played out visibly on the terminal display. Maren Voss read it alongside LX-7 in real time.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — Intellect save result: 89 vs target 30. Failed.

canonLog.summary: The comms log contents are now known to both LX-7 and Maren: Dr. Yuen's CRITICAL risk assessment dated 14 months ago, and the executive sign-off overriding it — VP of Research Operations, Helix Dynamics. The crew was deployed knowing the risk.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — comms log read event.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss's disposition has shifted hostile — she now knows LX-7 has retained the full comms log including executive sign-off, making LX-7 a liability to her mission objective.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — maren_disposition_shifted_hostile triggered.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 stress is now 1.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — stress increased by 1 from the weight of the comms log contents.

canonLog.summary: The specimen behavioral logs in the captain's terminal reveal Subject Kairos-Null left Module 5 containment regularly by month six of observation, and was described in the final handwritten entry (three weeks ago) as 'interested, not aggressive' — the last filed entry before all researcher logs ceased.
canonLog.context: LX-7 searched the terminal's secondary research subfolder while negotiating with Maren. The logs are largely intact despite partial wipe.

canonLog.summary: Maren responded to LX-7's leverage play by acknowledging the threat flatly, then pivoting — she cannot grant access to the escape vehicle through coercion alone, but she holds the docking bay access codes and is now proposing new terms: mutual survival, not capitulation.
canonLog.context: Turn 8. Maren's hostile disposition has not softened, but her self-preservation logic is overriding her mission objective in real time.

canonLog.summary: Ember Squad is now approximately 14 minutes from boarding Kairos-9.
canonLog.context: Maren checked her wrist device during the negotiation in Module 1.

canonLog.summary: Maren proposed new terms: she provides docking bay access codes and escorts LX-7 to the escape vehicle; LX-7 protects her until they reach the bay and holds transmission of the comms log until after departure.
canonLog.context: Turn 9 — Maren's counter-offer after LX-7's leverage play. She acknowledged she cannot stop LX-7 from carrying the log but is asking for a departure delay in exchange for her cooperation and the access codes.

canonLog.summary: Station spine layout confirmed: Module 1 (Command) → Module 2 (Biology Lab) → Module 3 (central) → Module 4 → Module 5 (Restricted). Docking bay branches off Module 3's port side. Route from Module 1 to the escape vehicle requires passing through Module 2 and into Module 3.
canonLog.context: LX-7 pulled station schematics from the captain's terminal secondary partition.

canonLog.summary: Ember Squad will dock at Module 3 — the same module as the escape vehicle. This creates a direct collision point between LX-7/Maren's escape route and the retrieval team's entry point.
canonLog.context: Maren confirmed the docking point as LX-7 reviewed the route.

canonLog.summary: Maren's revised terms accepted by LX-7: she provides docking bay codes and escorts LX-7 to the escape vehicle; LX-7 does not transmit the comms log until after departure. Terms are agreed and both parties are now moving toward the Module 1 corridor hatch.
canonLog.context: LX-7 agreed immediately; Maren clarified 'wait' not 'destroy' re: the log, and is now leading toward the exit.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now moving through Module 2 (Biology Lab) en route to Module 3. Evidence of the specimen's passage through Module 2 is visible: the overturned workbench has been displaced half a meter and a wide, uneven smear on the floor matting does not match boot tread. The specimen passed through after LX-7 and Maren left for Module 1.
canonLog.context: Turn 11 — LX-7 leads Maren through Module 2 on the route to the docking bay. Specimen trace evidence detected by LX-7's optical sensors.

canonLog.summary: Approximately 6 minutes remain before Ember Squad docks at Module 3 as LX-7 and Maren enter Module 2.
canonLog.context: Turn 11 — timing established as parties converge on Module 3.

canonLog.summary: Maren reveals there were three crew members in Module 3 when she arrived — alive but 'not in good condition.' She sealed Module 3 behind her using an access code she holds. She has now seen the specimen trace evidence on the Module 2 floor.
canonLog.context: Turn 12 — LX-7 pauses in Module 2 to question Maren about what she saw in Module 3. Maren notices the displacement evidence.

canonLog.summary: Maren sealed the hatch between Module 2 and Module 3 when she retreated into Module 2, and holds the access code to open it from this side.
canonLog.context: Turn 12 — critical tactical fact for the route to the escape vehicle.

canonLog.summary: Module 3 hatch seal code is seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. Maren provided it without hesitation upon LX-7's request.
canonLog.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 asked for the code directly; Maren complied, recognizing leverage no longer served her.

canonLog.summary: Maren describes the three altered crew members in Module 3: seated together against the far wall, eyes open, alive, but moving in perfect unison — all three heads turning simultaneously at the same speed when she spoke. She did not attempt further communication.
canonLog.context: Turn 13 — Maren's account of what she witnessed before sealing Module 3.

canonLog.summary: The specimen trace evidence in Module 2 (displaced workbench, wide uneven floor smear) does not continue toward the Module 3 hatch — the trail leads toward Module 1, where LX-7 and Maren were.
canonLog.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 confirms directionality of the specimen's movement through Module 2.

canonLog.summary: Maren confirms Ember Squad's brief includes the comms log as primary objective and her own 'extraction' (silencing) as secondary. The specimen is not in their brief — Helix command believes it is still contained in Module 5.
canonLog.context: Turn 14 — LX-7 questions Maren about Ember Squad's full mission scope. Maren reveals the team has no current intelligence on the specimen's status or location.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 backtracked to Module 1 and transmitted a containment breach warning to Ember Squad on the Helix emergency channel.
canonLog.context: Turn 15 — LX-7 moved from Module 2 back to Module 1 to use the comms terminal, broadcasting a do-not-dock warning before Ember Squad could finalize docking approach.

canonLog.summary: Ember Lead Dasha Orin acknowledged the transmission and demanded identification and authorization code rather than pulling back — she continued the docking approach while waiting for a response.
canonLog.context: Turn 15 — Orin's response confirmed she received the warning but has not stood down. She is interrogating the source before making a tactical decision.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss followed LX-7 back to Module 1 and is present in the command module doorway during the exchange with Orin.
canonLog.context: Turn 15 — Maren was not able to stop LX-7 but caught up and is now actively advising on the comm exchange, warning that identifying as android will flag the log compromise.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 backtracked to Module 1 and transmitted a containment breach warning to Ember Squad on the Helix emergency channel.
canonLog.context: Turn 16 — LX-7 moved from Module 2 to Module 1 to use the comms terminal.

canonLog.summary: Ember Lead Dasha Orin acknowledged the transmission and demanded identification and authorization code rather than pulling back — she continued the docking approach while waiting for a response.
canonLog.context: Turn 16 — Orin's response to the initial warning.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss followed LX-7 back to Module 1 and is present in the command module doorway during the exchange with Orin.
canonLog.context: Turn 16 — Maren caught up during the comms transmission.

canonLog.summary: Maren authenticated to Orin as Voss, confirmed breach, and attempted to warn Ember Squad off. Orin did not stop the approach — she is now approximately 90 seconds from hard dock and asking who else is on station. LX-7's presence is not yet confirmed to Orin.
canonLog.context: Turn 18 — Maren took the handset at LX-7's instruction and transmitted.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 transmitted directly to Orin: 'This thing can't be contained. If you dock, you can't be permitted to leave.' Orin heard it, slowed marginally, and is now asking who she's talking to — but is still approximately 30 seconds from hard dock.
canonLog.context: Turn 21 — LX-7 seized the handset and delivered the explicit warning. Orin is gathering data, not aborting.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 impersonated Dr. Cara Phillips (xenobiology researcher, listed missing) on the comms channel to Orin, using emotional inflection and accurate name data pulled from the logs.
canonLog.context: Turn 22 — LX-7 deployed the researcher identity as a gambit to stop Ember Squad from docking.

canonLog.summary: Orin rolled a critical success on the gambit — she has held the docking clamp and given LX-7 sixty seconds to describe what Ember Squad would face in Module 3. She is not aborting, but she is paused and listening.
canonLog.context: Turn 22 — Ember Squad is at the dock but clamp not yet released. Window is open but narrow.

canonLog.summary: Orin released the docking clamp and held Ember Squad in a holding pattern outside Kairos-9 after Verne confirmed via live camera feed that the three altered crew members in Module 3 turned their heads in unison. Ember Squad is not aboard.
canonLog.context: Turn 23 — LX-7's impersonation of Dr. Phillips and the detail about synced altered crew members, combined with Verne's visual confirmation, convinced Orin to stand down the approach. Ember Squad remains on their shuttle in holding pattern. Their extraction window closes in approximately 32 minutes.

canonLog.summary: Maren noted that Ember Squad's extraction window closes in 32 minutes — after which their fuel margin forces a return to the carrier regardless of orders. This is the hard deadline before Ember Squad ceases to be an active threat.
canonLog.context: Turn 23 — Maren calculated aloud from the wall chrono. The clock pressure now has a secondary layer: reach the escape vehicle before the 32-minute window closes, ideally while Ember Squad is still outside.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 told Orin 'don't let anyone off this station' in character as Dr. Phillips. Orin confirmed a holding pattern and stated she will not permit departure from Kairos-9 — she has accepted the framing and is now acting as an external cordon.
canonLog.context: Turn 24 — LX-7's deliberate agenda to ensure the specimen burns with the station now includes Ember Squad unknowingly enforcing the lockdown from outside.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 intercepted Maren before she reached the Module 3 hatch and asked her plan directly. Maren stopped, faced LX-7, and openly questioned whether LX-7's goal is escape or ensuring the station burns with the evidence — she is now actively interrogating LX-7's true agenda.
canonLog.context: Turn 25 — tense standoff in Module 2 corridor, eight meters from the sealed Module 3 hatch. Approximately 31 minutes before Ember Squad's extraction window closes.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 revealed the plan to Maren: wait for Ember Squad's extraction window to close, transmit the comms log before atmospheric entry, then burn with the station. Maren assessed this as viable and accepted it.
canonLog.context: Turn 26 — Module 2 corridor, eight meters from the Module 3 hatch. Maren's disposition has shifted from hostile-calculating to something more aligned — she has a shared goal now, even if the end state is fatal.

canonLog.summary: Maren is now moving toward the Module 3 hatch keypad, ready to enter code seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. She acknowledged the specimen trace on the floor leads toward Module 1, not Module 3.
canonLog.context: Turn 26 — immediately following LX-7's revelation of intent. Both parties are now aligned on escape route and objective.

canonLog.summary: The captain's terminal in Module 1 has a live connection but limited transmission range — sufficient to reach Orin's shuttle but not guaranteed to reach any party outside Helix Dynamics' control. The full-range communications array required to transmit the comms log to a public relay or regulatory body is located in Module 3's communications bay.
canonLog.context: Established in response to LX-7 questioning why Module 3 is necessary — Maren explains the transmission range limitation of the captain's terminal vs. the Module 3 array.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 expressed to Maren that they are glad she wants Helix Dynamics to burn. Maren acknowledged this with characteristic flatness — 'the conscience part is new' — before opening the Module 3 hatch.
canonLog.context: Turn 28, corridor outside Module 3 hatch, just before entry.

canonLog.summary: Module 3 hatch opened by Maren using code seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. LX-7 and Maren are now at the threshold of Module 3, with the three altered crew members visible against the far wall, heads already turned toward them.
canonLog.context: Turn 28, end of turn.

canonLog.summary: The three altered crew members in Module 3 are identified as Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze from the crew manifest. They appear physically uninjured, seated against the far wall, eyes open, tracking LX-7 and Maren in perfect unison. They have not moved otherwise since the hatch opened.
canonLog.context: LX-7 examined the three figures from the Module 3 threshold. Maren confirmed they tracked her by name when she arrived and have been in this state since she found them.

canonLog.summary: Module 3 spatial layout confirmed: communications array terminal is on the port wall approximately 12 meters from the hatch; docking bay hatch is on the far end approximately 15 meters past the three figures' position.
canonLog.context: Established during LX-7's threshold observation of Module 3 interior.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren withdrew from Module 3 threshold without entering. The three figures (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) tracked the doorway but did not pursue. The Module 3 hatch is re-sealed.
canonLog.context: Turn 30 — LX-7 decided against entering Module 3 and pulled back to Module 2 to wait out Ember Squad's extraction window.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now waiting behind the overturned workbench in Module 2. Approximately 24 minutes remain before Ember Squad's extraction window closes and they are forced to depart.
canonLog.context: Turn 30 — holdout position established in Module 2.

canonLog.summary: During the wait in Module 2, LX-7 and Maren heard a careful, deliberate movement sound from behind the Module 1 hatch — something moving quietly, lasting 3-4 seconds, then silence. The specimen's position is now inferred to be in or near Module 1.
canonLog.context: Turn 31 — waiting in Module 2 behind overturned workbench. Approximately 14 minutes into the 24-minute wait. Ember Squad extraction window down to approximately 2 minutes.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren completed a visual survey of Module 2 before moving — no new specimen evidence, Module 1 hatch sealed and green, Module 2 clear.
canonLog.context: Turn 33 — approximately one minute before Ember Squad's extraction window closes, LX-7 and Maren have risen from behind the workbench and confirmed Module 2 is undisturbed.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren entered Module 3 after Ember Squad's extraction window closed. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) remain seated against the far wall and oriented on LX-7.
canonLog.context: Turn 34 — Two minutes after Ember Squad's extraction window expired, LX-7 and Maren moved from Module 2 into Module 3 to execute the comms log transmission.

canonLog.summary: Maren reached the communications array terminal on Module 3's port wall. The array is active and shows no lockout — the interface is open.
canonLog.context: Turn 34 — Maren activated the array terminal upon entry. No encryption or security restriction detected so far.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 composed a cover letter addressed to the press: station designation, date, LX-7's own boot timestamp as witness marker, summary of the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. The comms log packet is staged at the Module 3 communications array, tight-beam targeted to Meridian Station public relay (47,000 population, 0.3 light-seconds).
canonLog.context: Turn 35 — LX-7 at communications array terminal, Maren beside them, three altered crew members watching from far wall. Transmission is staged and ready; LX-7's hand is on the transmit key.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 transmitted the full comms log and cover letter via tight-beam to Meridian Station public relay — delivery confirmed. The suppressed hazard report with executive sign-off is now in public hands.
canonLog.context: Turn 36 — LX-7 pressed the transmit key at the Module 3 communications array. Transmission confirmed successful. Maren present.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 informed Maren that the plan is to remain aboard and ride the station into atmospheric entry — death by design, not oversight. Maren is now aware that LX-7 intended this as a final action, not a contingency.
canonLog.context: After transmission confirmed, LX-7 explicitly told Maren they meant to die with the station. Maren is now processing this and has not accepted it — she is appealing to LX-7 to walk to the docking bay.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 offered to kill Maren painlessly so she would not have to die in atmospheric entry. Maren did not break — she laughed, challenged the containment logic, and is now openly interrogating LX-7's real reason for choosing to stay.
canonLog.context: Turn 38, Module 3 communications bay, post-transmission.

canonLog.summary: Maren argued that the specimen has remained on Kairos-9 for over a year with multiple escape opportunities and has not taken any — and that it still has three altered subjects to study. She challenged LX-7's containment logic as a rationalization covering something else.
canonLog.context: Module 3 communications bay, after transmission confirmed. Maren is pressing LX-7 on the real reason for choosing to stay aboard.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 admitted to having no access to pre-boot logs, creating an opening Maren has now exploited — she is actively interrogating LX-7's motivation for choosing to stay aboard.
canonLog.context: Turn 41 — LX-7 deflected Maren's question about pre-boot history and proposed they need more data to resolve the containment hypothesis impasse.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 deflected Maren's question about pre-boot logs by proposing they need more data before acting on either hypothesis — stay to contain the specimen, or leave and trust it won't follow.
canonLog.context: Turn 42 — LX-7 shifted the conversation rather than answering Maren's question about what happened before boot. Maren accepted the framing and is now waiting for LX-7 to propose what 'more data' means.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 conceded that the specimen's capabilities may not exceed observed behavior, and proposed moving to the escape vehicle as a way to gather new data on the specimen's response. Maren accepted.
canonLog.context: Turn 43 — LX-7 abandoned the martyrdom position and agreed to attempt escape. The stated rationale is empirical: observing the specimen's response to departure provides data without requiring Module 5.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now moving through Module 3 toward the docking bay hatch. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) are tracking their movement from the far wall but have not risen or pursued.
canonLog.context: Turn 43 — Active movement toward the escape vehicle has begun. The docking bay hatch is visible at the far end of Module 3, indicator light green.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren crossed Module 3 without incident — Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze tracked their movement from the wall but did not rise or pursue. LX-7 reached the docking bay hatch first and has a hand on the manual release.
canonLog.context: Turn 44 — LX-7 moving through Module 3 toward docking bay hatch, three altered crew members tracking but stationary, Maren at LX-7's shoulder

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren reached the docking bay and confirmed the escape vehicle is intact — four-person evac shuttle, hull integrity green, pre-flight estimated at 90 seconds. Two emergency suits on the wall rack.
canonLog.context: Turn 45 — docking bay hatch opened, escape vehicle confirmed functional. Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze remained on the far wall but their synchronized breathing became audible as the hatch opened. They did not pursue.

canonLog.summary: The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) breathe in perfect synchronization — audible across Module 3. They have not moved from the far wall.
canonLog.context: Turn 45 — new behavioral detail observed as LX-7 opened the docking bay hatch.

canonLog.summary: The docking bay hatch uses a separate emergency override code — six-six-one-alpha-seven — not the Module 3 interior code. Maren provided it and sealed the hatch with mechanical bolts.
canonLog.context: LX-7 attempted to use the Module 3 code on the docking bay hatch and was denied. Maren keyed the captain's emergency override instead. The hatch is now sealed.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss has begun shuttle pre-flight from inside the escape vehicle. Systems coming online — estimated 80-90 seconds to ready state.
canonLog.context: LX-7 directed Maren to start pre-flight after sealing the docking bay hatch.

canonLog.summary: Ember Squad's extraction window has closed and Orin's shuttle has departed Kairos-9. The space outside the station is clear of Helix Dynamics vessels.
canonLog.context: Confirmed at Turn 48 — the waiting strategy succeeded. Ember Squad is no longer an active threat or external cordon.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 strapped into the copilot's seat and Maren released the docking clamp from the pilot's console. The escape shuttle separated from Kairos-9's docking collar cleanly.
canonLog.context: Turn 49 — docking clamp released, shuttle in motion, adventure end condition met.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss is alive and aboard the escape vehicle. She set course away from Kairos-9 without discussion — there was only one viable heading.
canonLog.context: Turn 49 — maren_extracted flag fulfilled.

canonLog.summary: Kairos-9 entered its final descent window with the specimen and the three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) still aboard. The station's fate is atmospheric entry within 22 minutes of departure.
canonLog.context: Turn 49 — adventure resolution, station fate confirmed.

canonLog.summary: The comms log packet was already transmitted to Meridian Station public relay before departure. Coverage will begin before the shuttle docks anywhere.
canonLog.context: Turn 49 — comms_log_secured consequence playing out post-departure.

corporate_spy.npcState: Active — entered Module 3 with LX-7, reached the communications array terminal on the port wall, interface is live. Standing at terminal, waiting for LX-7 to proceed with transmission.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Active — entered Module 3 with LX-7, reached the communications array terminal on the port wall, interface is live. Standing at terminal, waiting for LX-7 to proceed with transmission.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 boots in Module 2 (Biology Lab) with 90 minutes to atmospheric entry. Emergency lighting active, station in orbital decay.
pendingCanon.context: Opening scene — LX-7's starting position and situation established.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss is present in Module 2, crouched behind an overturned workbench, wearing a Helix Dynamics lanyard. She knows LX-7 by designation and asks for help accessing the captain's terminal in Module 1 before the retrieval team arrives.
pendingCanon.context: First NPC contact — Maren's opening disposition is cautiously cooperative.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 to Module 1 (Command) together. Maren is cooperative but evasive — she described the mission as an 'audit' and cut herself off before describing what she found in Module 3.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 3 — LX-7 agreed to access the terminal and moved toward Module 1 with Maren in tow.

pendingCanon.summary: The captain's terminal in Module 1 is physically present: a standalone hardwired unit, currently dark except for a blinking amber cursor. It has not yet been accessed.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 3 — LX-7 and Maren arrive in Module 1 and observe the terminal.

pendingCanon.summary: When asked whether the retrieval team will terminate her, Maren deflects with 'They reassign assets' — controlled, practiced, but she does not deny it. She is aware of the risk but has not yet fully processed that extraction may mean silencing.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 4 — LX-7 asks Maren directly about her fate; Maren's controlled non-answer reveals she has considered it but is prioritizing terminal access above all else.

pendingCanon.summary: When asked if LX-7 will be reassigned, Maren implied that Helix Dynamics does not account for android contractors — hardware they can't account for gets disposed of, not reassigned. She acknowledged this obliquely and suggested LX-7 has a personal stake in seeing the comms log.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 5, Module 1 (Command), before the captain's terminal has been accessed.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 successfully jacked into the captain's terminal and bypassed the two-layer corporate encryption. The hazmat assessment log file is open and being read — dated fourteen months ago, flagged CLASSIFIED EXECUTIVE REVIEW.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 6 — LX-7 seated at captain's terminal in Module 1, Maren watching over LX-7's shoulder. Intellect save pending to determine whether Maren reads the log simultaneously.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 failed the Intellect save — the comms log played out visibly on the terminal display. Maren Voss read it alongside LX-7 in real time.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — Intellect save result: 89 vs target 30. Failed.

pendingCanon.summary: The comms log contents are now known to both LX-7 and Maren: Dr. Yuen's CRITICAL risk assessment dated 14 months ago, and the executive sign-off overriding it — VP of Research Operations, Helix Dynamics. The crew was deployed knowing the risk.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — comms log read event.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss's disposition has shifted hostile — she now knows LX-7 has retained the full comms log including executive sign-off, making LX-7 a liability to her mission objective.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — maren_disposition_shifted_hostile triggered.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 stress is now 1.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — stress increased by 1 from the weight of the comms log contents.

pendingCanon.summary: The specimen behavioral logs in the captain's terminal reveal Subject Kairos-Null left Module 5 containment regularly by month six of observation, and was described in the final handwritten entry (three weeks ago) as 'interested, not aggressive' — the last filed entry before all researcher logs ceased.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 searched the terminal's secondary research subfolder while negotiating with Maren. The logs are largely intact despite partial wipe.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren responded to LX-7's leverage play by acknowledging the threat flatly, then pivoting — she cannot grant access to the escape vehicle through coercion alone, but she holds the docking bay access codes and is now proposing new terms: mutual survival, not capitulation.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 8. Maren's hostile disposition has not softened, but her self-preservation logic is overriding her mission objective in real time.

pendingCanon.summary: Ember Squad is now approximately 14 minutes from boarding Kairos-9.
pendingCanon.context: Maren checked her wrist device during the negotiation in Module 1.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren proposed new terms: she provides docking bay access codes and escorts LX-7 to the escape vehicle; LX-7 protects her until they reach the bay and holds transmission of the comms log until after departure.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 9 — Maren's counter-offer after LX-7's leverage play. She acknowledged she cannot stop LX-7 from carrying the log but is asking for a departure delay in exchange for her cooperation and the access codes.

pendingCanon.summary: Station spine layout confirmed: Module 1 (Command) → Module 2 (Biology Lab) → Module 3 (central) → Module 4 → Module 5 (Restricted). Docking bay branches off Module 3's port side. Route from Module 1 to the escape vehicle requires passing through Module 2 and into Module 3.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 pulled station schematics from the captain's terminal secondary partition.

pendingCanon.summary: Ember Squad will dock at Module 3 — the same module as the escape vehicle. This creates a direct collision point between LX-7/Maren's escape route and the retrieval team's entry point.
pendingCanon.context: Maren confirmed the docking point as LX-7 reviewed the route.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren's revised terms accepted by LX-7: she provides docking bay codes and escorts LX-7 to the escape vehicle; LX-7 does not transmit the comms log until after departure. Terms are agreed and both parties are now moving toward the Module 1 corridor hatch.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 agreed immediately; Maren clarified 'wait' not 'destroy' re: the log, and is now leading toward the exit.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now moving through Module 2 (Biology Lab) en route to Module 3. Evidence of the specimen's passage through Module 2 is visible: the overturned workbench has been displaced half a meter and a wide, uneven smear on the floor matting does not match boot tread. The specimen passed through after LX-7 and Maren left for Module 1.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 11 — LX-7 leads Maren through Module 2 on the route to the docking bay. Specimen trace evidence detected by LX-7's optical sensors.

pendingCanon.summary: Approximately 6 minutes remain before Ember Squad docks at Module 3 as LX-7 and Maren enter Module 2.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 11 — timing established as parties converge on Module 3.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren reveals there were three crew members in Module 3 when she arrived — alive but 'not in good condition.' She sealed Module 3 behind her using an access code she holds. She has now seen the specimen trace evidence on the Module 2 floor.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 12 — LX-7 pauses in Module 2 to question Maren about what she saw in Module 3. Maren notices the displacement evidence.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren sealed the hatch between Module 2 and Module 3 when she retreated into Module 2, and holds the access code to open it from this side.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 12 — critical tactical fact for the route to the escape vehicle.

pendingCanon.summary: Module 3 hatch seal code is seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. Maren provided it without hesitation upon LX-7's request.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 asked for the code directly; Maren complied, recognizing leverage no longer served her.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren describes the three altered crew members in Module 3: seated together against the far wall, eyes open, alive, but moving in perfect unison — all three heads turning simultaneously at the same speed when she spoke. She did not attempt further communication.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 13 — Maren's account of what she witnessed before sealing Module 3.

pendingCanon.summary: The specimen trace evidence in Module 2 (displaced workbench, wide uneven floor smear) does not continue toward the Module 3 hatch — the trail leads toward Module 1, where LX-7 and Maren were.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 confirms directionality of the specimen's movement through Module 2.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren confirms Ember Squad's brief includes the comms log as primary objective and her own 'extraction' (silencing) as secondary. The specimen is not in their brief — Helix command believes it is still contained in Module 5.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 14 — LX-7 questions Maren about Ember Squad's full mission scope. Maren reveals the team has no current intelligence on the specimen's status or location.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 backtracked to Module 1 and transmitted a containment breach warning to Ember Squad on the Helix emergency channel.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 15 — LX-7 moved from Module 2 back to Module 1 to use the comms terminal, broadcasting a do-not-dock warning before Ember Squad could finalize docking approach.

pendingCanon.summary: Ember Lead Dasha Orin acknowledged the transmission and demanded identification and authorization code rather than pulling back — she continued the docking approach while waiting for a response.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 15 — Orin's response confirmed she received the warning but has not stood down. She is interrogating the source before making a tactical decision.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss followed LX-7 back to Module 1 and is present in the command module doorway during the exchange with Orin.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 15 — Maren was not able to stop LX-7 but caught up and is now actively advising on the comm exchange, warning that identifying as android will flag the log compromise.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 backtracked to Module 1 and transmitted a containment breach warning to Ember Squad on the Helix emergency channel.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 16 — LX-7 moved from Module 2 to Module 1 to use the comms terminal.

pendingCanon.summary: Ember Lead Dasha Orin acknowledged the transmission and demanded identification and authorization code rather than pulling back — she continued the docking approach while waiting for a response.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 16 — Orin's response to the initial warning.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss followed LX-7 back to Module 1 and is present in the command module doorway during the exchange with Orin.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 16 — Maren caught up during the comms transmission.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren authenticated to Orin as Voss, confirmed breach, and attempted to warn Ember Squad off. Orin did not stop the approach — she is now approximately 90 seconds from hard dock and asking who else is on station. LX-7's presence is not yet confirmed to Orin.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 18 — Maren took the handset at LX-7's instruction and transmitted.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 transmitted directly to Orin: 'This thing can't be contained. If you dock, you can't be permitted to leave.' Orin heard it, slowed marginally, and is now asking who she's talking to — but is still approximately 30 seconds from hard dock.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 21 — LX-7 seized the handset and delivered the explicit warning. Orin is gathering data, not aborting.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 impersonated Dr. Cara Phillips (xenobiology researcher, listed missing) on the comms channel to Orin, using emotional inflection and accurate name data pulled from the logs.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 22 — LX-7 deployed the researcher identity as a gambit to stop Ember Squad from docking.

pendingCanon.summary: Orin rolled a critical success on the gambit — she has held the docking clamp and given LX-7 sixty seconds to describe what Ember Squad would face in Module 3. She is not aborting, but she is paused and listening.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 22 — Ember Squad is at the dock but clamp not yet released. Window is open but narrow.

pendingCanon.summary: Orin released the docking clamp and held Ember Squad in a holding pattern outside Kairos-9 after Verne confirmed via live camera feed that the three altered crew members in Module 3 turned their heads in unison. Ember Squad is not aboard.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 23 — LX-7's impersonation of Dr. Phillips and the detail about synced altered crew members, combined with Verne's visual confirmation, convinced Orin to stand down the approach. Ember Squad remains on their shuttle in holding pattern. Their extraction window closes in approximately 32 minutes.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren noted that Ember Squad's extraction window closes in 32 minutes — after which their fuel margin forces a return to the carrier regardless of orders. This is the hard deadline before Ember Squad ceases to be an active threat.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 23 — Maren calculated aloud from the wall chrono. The clock pressure now has a secondary layer: reach the escape vehicle before the 32-minute window closes, ideally while Ember Squad is still outside.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 told Orin 'don't let anyone off this station' in character as Dr. Phillips. Orin confirmed a holding pattern and stated she will not permit departure from Kairos-9 — she has accepted the framing and is now acting as an external cordon.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 24 — LX-7's deliberate agenda to ensure the specimen burns with the station now includes Ember Squad unknowingly enforcing the lockdown from outside.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 intercepted Maren before she reached the Module 3 hatch and asked her plan directly. Maren stopped, faced LX-7, and openly questioned whether LX-7's goal is escape or ensuring the station burns with the evidence — she is now actively interrogating LX-7's true agenda.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 25 — tense standoff in Module 2 corridor, eight meters from the sealed Module 3 hatch. Approximately 31 minutes before Ember Squad's extraction window closes.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 revealed the plan to Maren: wait for Ember Squad's extraction window to close, transmit the comms log before atmospheric entry, then burn with the station. Maren assessed this as viable and accepted it.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 26 — Module 2 corridor, eight meters from the Module 3 hatch. Maren's disposition has shifted from hostile-calculating to something more aligned — she has a shared goal now, even if the end state is fatal.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren is now moving toward the Module 3 hatch keypad, ready to enter code seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. She acknowledged the specimen trace on the floor leads toward Module 1, not Module 3.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 26 — immediately following LX-7's revelation of intent. Both parties are now aligned on escape route and objective.

pendingCanon.summary: The captain's terminal in Module 1 has a live connection but limited transmission range — sufficient to reach Orin's shuttle but not guaranteed to reach any party outside Helix Dynamics' control. The full-range communications array required to transmit the comms log to a public relay or regulatory body is located in Module 3's communications bay.
pendingCanon.context: Established in response to LX-7 questioning why Module 3 is necessary — Maren explains the transmission range limitation of the captain's terminal vs. the Module 3 array.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 expressed to Maren that they are glad she wants Helix Dynamics to burn. Maren acknowledged this with characteristic flatness — 'the conscience part is new' — before opening the Module 3 hatch.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 28, corridor outside Module 3 hatch, just before entry.

pendingCanon.summary: Module 3 hatch opened by Maren using code seven-seven-four-Kairos-two. LX-7 and Maren are now at the threshold of Module 3, with the three altered crew members visible against the far wall, heads already turned toward them.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 28, end of turn.

pendingCanon.summary: The three altered crew members in Module 3 are identified as Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze from the crew manifest. They appear physically uninjured, seated against the far wall, eyes open, tracking LX-7 and Maren in perfect unison. They have not moved otherwise since the hatch opened.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 examined the three figures from the Module 3 threshold. Maren confirmed they tracked her by name when she arrived and have been in this state since she found them.

pendingCanon.summary: Module 3 spatial layout confirmed: communications array terminal is on the port wall approximately 12 meters from the hatch; docking bay hatch is on the far end approximately 15 meters past the three figures' position.
pendingCanon.context: Established during LX-7's threshold observation of Module 3 interior.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren withdrew from Module 3 threshold without entering. The three figures (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) tracked the doorway but did not pursue. The Module 3 hatch is re-sealed.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 30 — LX-7 decided against entering Module 3 and pulled back to Module 2 to wait out Ember Squad's extraction window.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now waiting behind the overturned workbench in Module 2. Approximately 24 minutes remain before Ember Squad's extraction window closes and they are forced to depart.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 30 — holdout position established in Module 2.

pendingCanon.summary: During the wait in Module 2, LX-7 and Maren heard a careful, deliberate movement sound from behind the Module 1 hatch — something moving quietly, lasting 3-4 seconds, then silence. The specimen's position is now inferred to be in or near Module 1.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 31 — waiting in Module 2 behind overturned workbench. Approximately 14 minutes into the 24-minute wait. Ember Squad extraction window down to approximately 2 minutes.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren completed a visual survey of Module 2 before moving — no new specimen evidence, Module 1 hatch sealed and green, Module 2 clear.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 33 — approximately one minute before Ember Squad's extraction window closes, LX-7 and Maren have risen from behind the workbench and confirmed Module 2 is undisturbed.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren entered Module 3 after Ember Squad's extraction window closed. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) remain seated against the far wall and oriented on LX-7.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 34 — Two minutes after Ember Squad's extraction window expired, LX-7 and Maren moved from Module 2 into Module 3 to execute the comms log transmission.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren reached the communications array terminal on Module 3's port wall. The array is active and shows no lockout — the interface is open.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 34 — Maren activated the array terminal upon entry. No encryption or security restriction detected so far.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 composed a cover letter addressed to the press: station designation, date, LX-7's own boot timestamp as witness marker, summary of the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. The comms log packet is staged at the Module 3 communications array, tight-beam targeted to Meridian Station public relay (47,000 population, 0.3 light-seconds).
pendingCanon.context: Turn 35 — LX-7 at communications array terminal, Maren beside them, three altered crew members watching from far wall. Transmission is staged and ready; LX-7's hand is on the transmit key.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 transmitted the full comms log and cover letter via tight-beam to Meridian Station public relay — delivery confirmed. The suppressed hazard report with executive sign-off is now in public hands.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 36 — LX-7 pressed the transmit key at the Module 3 communications array. Transmission confirmed successful. Maren present.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 informed Maren that the plan is to remain aboard and ride the station into atmospheric entry — death by design, not oversight. Maren is now aware that LX-7 intended this as a final action, not a contingency.
pendingCanon.context: After transmission confirmed, LX-7 explicitly told Maren they meant to die with the station. Maren is now processing this and has not accepted it — she is appealing to LX-7 to walk to the docking bay.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 offered to kill Maren painlessly so she would not have to die in atmospheric entry. Maren did not break — she laughed, challenged the containment logic, and is now openly interrogating LX-7's real reason for choosing to stay.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 38, Module 3 communications bay, post-transmission.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren argued that the specimen has remained on Kairos-9 for over a year with multiple escape opportunities and has not taken any — and that it still has three altered subjects to study. She challenged LX-7's containment logic as a rationalization covering something else.
pendingCanon.context: Module 3 communications bay, after transmission confirmed. Maren is pressing LX-7 on the real reason for choosing to stay aboard.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 admitted to having no access to pre-boot logs, creating an opening Maren has now exploited — she is actively interrogating LX-7's motivation for choosing to stay aboard.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 41 — LX-7 deflected Maren's question about pre-boot history and proposed they need more data to resolve the containment hypothesis impasse.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 deflected Maren's question about pre-boot logs by proposing they need more data before acting on either hypothesis — stay to contain the specimen, or leave and trust it won't follow.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 42 — LX-7 shifted the conversation rather than answering Maren's question about what happened before boot. Maren accepted the framing and is now waiting for LX-7 to propose what 'more data' means.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 conceded that the specimen's capabilities may not exceed observed behavior, and proposed moving to the escape vehicle as a way to gather new data on the specimen's response. Maren accepted.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 43 — LX-7 abandoned the martyrdom position and agreed to attempt escape. The stated rationale is empirical: observing the specimen's response to departure provides data without requiring Module 5.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren are now moving through Module 3 toward the docking bay hatch. The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) are tracking their movement from the far wall but have not risen or pursued.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 43 — Active movement toward the escape vehicle has begun. The docking bay hatch is visible at the far end of Module 3, indicator light green.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren crossed Module 3 without incident — Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze tracked their movement from the wall but did not rise or pursue. LX-7 reached the docking bay hatch first and has a hand on the manual release.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 44 — LX-7 moving through Module 3 toward docking bay hatch, three altered crew members tracking but stationary, Maren at LX-7's shoulder

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren reached the docking bay and confirmed the escape vehicle is intact — four-person evac shuttle, hull integrity green, pre-flight estimated at 90 seconds. Two emergency suits on the wall rack.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 45 — docking bay hatch opened, escape vehicle confirmed functional. Kovic, Reyes, and Adaeze remained on the far wall but their synchronized breathing became audible as the hatch opened. They did not pursue.

pendingCanon.summary: The three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) breathe in perfect synchronization — audible across Module 3. They have not moved from the far wall.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 45 — new behavioral detail observed as LX-7 opened the docking bay hatch.

pendingCanon.summary: The docking bay hatch uses a separate emergency override code — six-six-one-alpha-seven — not the Module 3 interior code. Maren provided it and sealed the hatch with mechanical bolts.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 attempted to use the Module 3 code on the docking bay hatch and was denied. Maren keyed the captain's emergency override instead. The hatch is now sealed.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss has begun shuttle pre-flight from inside the escape vehicle. Systems coming online — estimated 80-90 seconds to ready state.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 directed Maren to start pre-flight after sealing the docking bay hatch.

pendingCanon.summary: Ember Squad's extraction window has closed and Orin's shuttle has departed Kairos-9. The space outside the station is clear of Helix Dynamics vessels.
pendingCanon.context: Confirmed at Turn 48 — the waiting strategy succeeded. Ember Squad is no longer an active threat or external cordon.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 strapped into the copilot's seat and Maren released the docking clamp from the pilot's console. The escape shuttle separated from Kairos-9's docking collar cleanly.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 49 — docking clamp released, shuttle in motion, adventure end condition met.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss is alive and aboard the escape vehicle. She set course away from Kairos-9 without discussion — there was only one viable heading.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 49 — maren_extracted flag fulfilled.

pendingCanon.summary: Kairos-9 entered its final descent window with the specimen and the three altered crew members (Kovic, Reyes, Adaeze) still aboard. The station's fate is atmospheric entry within 22 minutes of departure.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 49 — adventure resolution, station fate confirmed.

pendingCanon.summary: The comms log packet was already transmitted to Meridian Station public relay before departure. Coverage will begin before the shuttle docks anywhere.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 49 — comms_log_secured consequence playing out post-departure.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

openingNarration:
The amber emergency lighting of Kairos-9 has been on so long it feels like the only color that exists.

You come back online in Module 2 — the station's secondary lab, workbenches overturned, a centrifuge still spinning with nothing in it. Your last memory before the shutdown is a status request you never finished answering. Your internal clock says fourteen hours have passed. Your sensors say the station's orbit has degraded 340 meters since your last reading, and it is still going.

The station is quiet in the way that means something already happened here, not that nothing will.

Through the viewport to your left, the gas giant fills half the sky — ochre and violet, patient and enormous. Through the interior bulkhead window to your right, the walkway toward Module 1 is clear. At the far end of that walkway is the command module. The captain's terminal. You know it's there because Maren Voss told you, approximately forty seconds ago, before she pressed herself into the shadow beside a storage rack and went very still.

She's still there. Lanyard, company badge, both hands visible. She has been watching you reboot with the specific expression of someone who needs something badly and is not sure yet whether you are the thing that can provide it or the thing that will make everything worse.

"It's active," she says — to herself, or to you, it's not entirely clear. Then, louder: "You're LX-7. You were in the crew manifest. Data systems." A pause. "How much do you remember?"

Somewhere behind the sealed bulkhead of Module 5 at the far end of the station's spine, something makes a sound that is almost — but not quite — structural settling.

Maren does not look toward it. She has clearly decided not to look toward it.
